corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: While the recent ViT/DeiT models abandon translation invariance in image classification even though it has long been shown to be crucial for visual modeling, we find that inductive bias that encourages certain translation invariance is still preferable for general-purpose visual modeling, par- ticularly for the dense prediction tasks of object detection and semantic segmentation.

context_window.previous: Also note that while the inclusion of absolute position embedding improves image classification accuracy (+0.4%), it harms object detection and semantic segmentation (-0.2 box/mask AP on COCO and -0.6 mIoU on ADE20K).

top_candidates.sentence: Reg-Net and EfficientNet , the Swin Transformer achieves a slightly better speed-accuracy trade-off.
The larger Swin-L model achieves 87.3% top-1 accuracy, +0.9% better than that of the Swin-B model. (a) Regular ImageNet-1K trained models method image size #param.

Swin-T with the shifted window partitioning outperforms the counterpart built on a single window partitioning at each stage by +1.1% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K, +2.8 box AP/+2.2 mask AP on COCO, and +2.8 mIoU on ADE20K.
Compared to the previous state-of-the-art Transformerbased architecture, i.e.
Compared with the state-of-the-art ConvNets, i.e.
An overview of the Swin Transformer architecture is presented in Figure , which illustrates the tiny version (Swin-T).
As a result, the proposed architecture can conveniently replace the backbone networks in existing methods for various vision tasks.
We also introduce Swin-T, Swin-S and Swin-L, which are versions of about 0.25×, 0.5× and 2× the model size and computational complexity, respectively.

The architecture hyper-parameters of these model variants are: • Swin-T: C = 96, layer numbers = {2, 2, 6, 2} • Swin-S: C = 96, layer numbers ={2, 2, 18, 2} • Swin-B: C = 128, layer numbers ={2, 2, 18, 2} • Swin-L: C = 192, layer numbers ={2, 2, 18, 2} where C is the channel number of the hidden layers in the first stage.

The detailed architecture specifications are shown in Table , where an input image size of 224×224 is assumed for all architectures. "Concat n × n" indicates a concatenation of n × n neighboring features in a patch.
Reg-Net and EfficientNet , the Swin Transformer achieves a slightly better speed-accuracy trade-off.

Compared with the previous best results for ImageNet-22K pre-training, our models achieve significantly better speed-accuracy trade-offs: Swin-B obtains 86.4% top-1 accuracy, which is 2.4% higher than that of ViT with similar inference throughput (84.7 vs.
The larger Swin-L model achieves 87.3% top-1 accuracy, +0.9% better than that of the Swin-B model. (a) Regular ImageNet-1K trained models method image size #param.

Swin-T with the shifted window partitioning outperforms the counterpart built on a single window partitioning at each stage by +1.1% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K, +2.8 box AP/+2.2 mask AP on COCO, and +2.8 mIoU on ADE20K.
To address these differences, we propose a hierarchical Transformer whose representation is computed with Shifted windows.
This hierarchical architecture has the flexibility to model at various scales and has linear computational complexity with respect to image size.

These qualities of Swin Transformer make it compatible with a broad range of vision tasks, including image classification (87.3 top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K) and dense prediction tasks such as object detection (58.7 box AP and 51.1 mask AP on COCO testdev) and semantic segmentation (53.5 mIoU on ADE20K val).
The hierarchical design and the shifted window approach also prove beneficial for all-MLP architectures.

Beginning with AlexNet and its revolutionary performance on the ImageNet image classification challenge, CNN architectures have evolved to become increasingly powerful through produce feature maps of a single low resolution and have quadratic computation complexity to input image size due to computation of selfattention globally. greater scale , more extensive connections , and more sophisticated forms of convolution .
On the other hand, the evolution of network architectures in natural language processing (NLP) has taken a different path, where the prevalent architecture today is instead the Transformer .
To overcome these issues, we propose a generalpurpose Transformer backbone, called Swin Transformer, which constructs hierarchical feature maps and has linear computational complexity to image size.
A key design element of Swin Transformer is its shift of the window partition between consecutive self-attention layers, as illustrated in Figure .
The proposed Swin Transformer achieves strong performance on the recognition tasks of image classification, object detection and semantic segmentation.
It outperforms the ViT / DeiT and ResNe(X)t models significantly with similar latency on the three tasks.
Its 58.7 box AP and 51.1 mask AP on the COCO test-dev set surpass the previous state-of-the-art results by +2.7 box AP (Copy-paste without external data) and +2.6 mask AP (DetectoRS ).

downstream_contract.intended_use: Feed this JSON to a later local open-source model to write an overview.
downstream_contract.grounding_rule: The downstream generator should write from evidence_units.location.sentence and context_window, not from unsupported outside knowledge.
downstream_contract.score_warning: Scores are ranking and filtering signals, not calibrated probabilities.
